message:
Implement full Amp and OpenCode connector log parsing

This commit transforms the Amp and OpenCode connectors from placeholder
stubs into fully functional conversation importers, enabling comprehensive
indexing of session history from both coding agents.

Amp Connector (amp.rs):
- Multi-root discovery: checks ~/.local/share/amp, VS Code globalStorage
  on Linux/macOS/Windows (config, Library/Application Support, AppData)
- Recursive JSON file scanning via walkdir with flexible naming detection
  (thread/conversation/chat in filename)
- Robust message extraction with multiple field aliases:
  - Role: role, speaker, type
  - Content: content, text, body
  - Timestamp: created_at, timestamp, ts
  - Author: author, sender
- Incremental indexing support via since_ts filtering
- Workspace inference from multiple metadata fields (workspace, cwd, path,
  project_path, repo, root)
- Deduplication across multiple JSON files using HashSet<String> keyed by
  external_id or file path
- Graceful fallback for missing metadata (title from first message, etc.)

OpenCode Connector (opencode.rs):
- Dynamic SQLite database discovery across:
  - Project-local: ./.opencode
  - User-global: ~/.opencode
  - XDG data dir: ~/.local/share/opencode, ~/.local/share/opencode/project
- Recursive .db/.sqlite file scanning with fuzzy name matching
  (database, storage, etc.)
- Schema introspection via PRAGMA table_info for resilient column mapping
- Multi-table correlation: sessions table (metadata) + messages table (content)
- Flexible column alias resolution for timestamps (created_at, timestamp, ts)
- Grouping messages by session_id or task_id with fallback to ungrouped pool
- Per-database deduplication to handle multiple DBs with overlapping IDs
- Chronological message sorting within conversations
- Safe error handling with per-DB failure isolation (logs warnings, continues)

Common improvements across both connectors:
- Message indexing (0-based idx field assignment after grouping)
- Timestamp-based incremental filtering (respects since_ts from ScanContext)
- Metadata preservation via serde_json::Value in extra field
- Consistent NormalizedConversation structure with started_at/ended_at ranges
- UTF-8 error tolerance (failed reads logged and skipped, not fatal)

These implementations enable the full connector matrix promised in README:
- Amp: sourcegraph.amp globalStorage + ~/.local/share/amp JSON caches
- OpenCode: .opencode SQLite DBs (project/local/global)

Watch mode (--watch) will now trigger incremental scans for both connectors
when their respective log files are modified, completing coverage for the
six supported coding agents (Codex, Cline, Gemini, Claude Code, OpenCode, Amp).

Testing notes:
- Amp connector tested against VS Code globalStorage JSON structure
- OpenCode connector tested against .opencode SQLite schema with sessions
  and messages tables
- Both connectors handle missing/malformed data gracefully (empty Vec return)
